content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(1, 5, 0), (1, 7, 2), (4, 7, 2), (0, 8, 4), (3, 6, 4), (2, 7, 9), (2, 4, 11), (5, 6, 11), (5, 9, 11), (4, 6, 12), (2, 5, 14), (6, 8, 14), (1, 2, 15), (3, 8, 15), (3, 9, 17)]

We need to find the nodes linked with node 5 exactly at time 0.

First, we will scan each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (1, 5, 0) has node 1 and time 0. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (1, 7, 2) has node 1 and time 2. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 7, 2) has node 4 and time 2. -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 8, 4) has node 0 and time 4. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 6, 4) has node 3 and time 4. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 7, 9) has node 2 and time 9. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 4, 11) has node 2 and time 11. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 6, 11) has node 5 and time 11. -> Ignore.
* Edge (5, 9, 11) has node 5 and time 11. -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 6, 12) has node 4 and time 12. -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 5, 14) has node 2 and time 14. -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 8, 14) has node 6 and time 14. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 2, 15) has node 1 and time 15. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 8, 15) has node 3 and time 15. -> Ignore.
* Edge (3, 9, 17) has node 3 and time 17. -> Ignore.

The nodes matched are 5.

Answer: [5]
